cannes.palme_dor.note: Ken Loach's second Palme d'Or (after The Wind That Shakes the Barley, 2006) — joins the small group of two-time Palme winners (Bille August, Emir Kusturica, Shohei Imamura, Michael Haneke, Dardennes, Loach; Östlund and Audiard later joined the group). Loach was 80 at the time of the win, one of the oldest Palme d'Or winners.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.winner_film: It's Only the End of the World
cannes.grand_prix.note: Xavier Dolan's second Grand Prix-equivalent prize — he had won the Jury Prize in 2014 for Mommy. Dolan was 27 at this win, one of the youngest filmmakers to receive the Grand Prix in modern Cannes history.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Andrea Arnold's third Jury Prize at Cannes (after Red Road, 2006; Fish Tank, 2009) — making her the most-credited Jury Prize recipient in Cannes history.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Two-way tie. Cristian Mungiu (Romania) shares Best Director with Olivier Assayas (France). Mungiu had previously won the Palme d'Or in 2007 for 4 Months, 3 Weeks and 2 Days. Both rows correctly flagged co_winner=true (sub-bullet tie format).
cannes.best_director.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Shahab Hosseini becomes the first Iranian actor to win Best Actor at Cannes. The Salesman went on to win Best International Feature at the 89th Academy Awards (cer 89) — Asghar Farhadi's second Oscar in the category.
cannes.best_actor.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Jaclyn Jose becomes the first Filipino actress to win Best Actress at Cannes.
cannes.best_actress.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: The Salesman wins both Best Actor (Hosseini) and Best Screenplay (Farhadi) — a rare double-honour for a single film at Cannes.
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Ruben Östlund's first Palme d'Or — he later won again in 2022 (Triangle of Sadness), making him a two-time Palme winner. The 70th Cannes Film Festival anniversary edition.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Robin Campillo's first Grand Prix — BPM is a film about the AIDS activism movement ACT UP Paris in the early 1990s.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Andrey Zvyagintsev's second Jury Prize (after Leviathan's Best Screenplay in 2014). Loveless went on to receive an Oscar nomination for Best International Feature (cer 90).
cannes.jury_prize.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Sofia Coppola becomes the second woman ever to win Best Director at Cannes (the first was Soviet filmmaker Yuliya Solntseva in 1961 — a 56-year gap).
cannes.best_director.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Joaquin Phoenix's first Cannes Best Actor — he later won Best Actor at the 92nd Academy Awards (cer 92) for Joker.
cannes.best_actor.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Diane Kruger's first Cannes Best Actress.
cannes.best_actress.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.winner_film: The Killing of a Sacred Deer
winner_films: The Killing of a Sacred Deer
cannes.best_screenplay.note: Two-way tie. Yorgos Lanthimos and Efthimis Filippou (The Killing of a Sacred Deer) share Best Screenplay with Lynne Ramsay (You Were Never Really Here). Both rows flagged co_winner=true. You Were Never Really Here also won Best Actor (Phoenix) at this ceremony — a rare same-film cross-category tied honour.
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Hirokazu Kore-eda's first Palme d'Or — Japan's first Palme d'Or since Shohei Imamura's The Eel in 1997 (a 21-year gap). Shoplifters went on to receive a Best International Feature nomination at the 91st Academy Awards (cer 91).
cannes.palme_dor.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Spike Lee's first major Cannes prize. BlacKkKlansman went on to win Best Adapted Screenplay at the 91st Academy Awards (cer 91) — Lee's first competitive Oscar after his 2016 Honorary Award.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Nadine Labaki's first Cannes prize — Lebanon's first major Cannes win. Capernaum went on to receive a Best International Feature nomination at the 91st Academy Awards (cer 91).
cannes.jury_prize.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Paweł Pawlikowski's first Cannes Best Director. Cold War went on to receive three Academy Award nominations (cer 91: Director, Cinematography, International Feature).
cannes.best_director.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Marcello Fonte's first major acting prize.
cannes.best_actor.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Samal Yeslyamova becomes the first Kazakh actress to win Best Actress at Cannes.
cannes.best_actress.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: Two-way tie. Alice Rohrwacher (Happy as Lazzaro) shares Best Screenplay with Jafar Panahi and Nader Saeivar (3 Faces). Panahi was barred from leaving Iran at the time; the prize was accepted on his behalf. Panahi later won the Palme d'Or in 2025 (his first festival appearance after the lifting of his travel ban). Both rows flagged co_winner=true.
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.note: Lukas Dhont's debut feature wins Caméra d'Or — Dhont later took the Grand Prix in 2022 for Close.
cannes.camera_dor.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.note: Manually inserted row — absent from the Wikipedia ceremony article. See known-issue cannes-2018-short-film-palme-dor-wikipedia-gap-2026-05-04. Source: festival-cannes.com / Variety. The truth-file landmark mirrors the canonical winner; Phase E winner_film and winner_recipient checks pass against the manually-inserted row.
cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article gap (manual insertion) / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Bong Joon-ho's first Palme d'Or — and South Korea's first Palme d'Or in the festival's history. Parasite went on to win Best Picture, Director, Original Screenplay, and International Feature at the 92nd Academy Awards (cer 92), becoming the first non-English-language film ever to win Best Picture in the 92-year history of the Oscars. The Cannes-to-Oscar trajectory is one of the most-celebrated awards seasons in modern cinema.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Mati Diop becomes the first Black woman to have a film selected in Competition at Cannes AND to win a major prize. Atlantics is a Senegalese-French co-production.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Three-way tie — the first 3-way Jury Prize tie in modern Cannes history (the second was the 2025 ceremony). Bacurau (Mendonça Filho/Dornelles), Les Misérables (Ladj Ly), and Fire Will Come (Oliver Laxe) share. CSV co_winner flag inconsistency: the third row (Fire Will Come) has co_winner=false because of the multi-bullet-tie pattern; the first two are correctly flagged. See known-issue cannes-co-winner-flag-multi-bullet-tie-2026-05-04.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Two-way tie. The Dardenne brothers (two-time Palme winners for Rosetta 1999 and L'Enfant 2005) share Best Director with Kantemir Balagov (Beanpole). CSV co_winner=false on both rows because of the multi-bullet-tie pattern; same root cause as 2025 Best Director tie. See known-issue cannes-co-winner-flag-multi-bullet-tie-2026-05-04.
cannes.best_director.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Antonio Banderas's first Cannes Best Actor — for Pedro Almodóvar's Pain and Glory. Banderas had been a long-running Almodóvar collaborator. He went on to receive a Best Actor Oscar nomination for the same role at the 92nd Academy Awards (cer 92).
cannes.best_actor.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Emily Beecham's first Cannes Best Actress.
cannes.best_actress.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.winner_film: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
cannes.best_screenplay.note: Céline Sciamma's first major Cannes prize.
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.winner_film: The Distance Between Us and the Sky
cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Julia Ducournau becomes the first woman to win the Palme d'Or solo (Jane Campion's 1993 win for The Piano was shared with Chen Kaige's Farewell My Concubine). Titane was widely considered a divisive choice; jury president Spike Lee mistakenly announced the winner at the start of the ceremony rather than the end. The 74th Cannes — postponed from May to July 2021 due to COVID-19 (replacing the 2020 cancellation).
cannes.palme_dor.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / Variety / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Two-way tie. Asghar Farhadi (Iran) shares Grand Prix with Juho Kuosmanen (Finland). Farhadi already held two International Feature Oscars at this point (A Separation 2012, The Salesman 2017).
cannes.grand_prix.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Two-way tie. Apichatpong Weerasethakul's Memoria shares Jury Prize with Nadav Lapid's Ahed's Knee — Weerasethakul previously won the Palme d'Or in 2010 for Uncle Boonmee.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Leos Carax's first Best Director Cannes prize. Annette opened the festival.
cannes.best_director.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Caleb Landry Jones's first major acting prize.
cannes.best_actor.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.winner_film: The Worst Person in the World
cannes.best_actress.note: Renate Reinsve becomes the first Norwegian actress to win Best Actress at Cannes. The Worst Person in the World was later nominated for two Oscars (cer 94: Best International Feature + Best Original Screenplay).
cannes.best_actress.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: Ryusuke Hamaguchi and Takamasa Oe win Best Screenplay for Drive My Car, which went on to win Best International Feature at the 94th Academy Awards (cer 94 in ORBIT Oscar truth) — the first Japanese film ever to win that Oscar category in the modern era after a long drought.
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.winner_film: All the Crows in the World
cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Ruben Östlund's second Palme d'Or (after The Square, 2017) — joins the small group of two-time Palme winners (Bille August, Emir Kusturica, Shohei Imamura, Michael Haneke, Ken Loach, Dardenne brothers).
cannes.palme_dor.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Two-way tie. Lukas Dhont (Close) shares Grand Prix with Claire Denis (Stars at Noon). Denis is one of the most-prolific Cannes alums; Stars at Noon her first Grand Prix.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Two-way tie. Jerzy Skolimowski's EO (a donkey-perspective road movie) shares with Felix van Groeningen and Charlotte Vandermeersch's The Eight Mountains.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Park Chan-wook's first Best Director at Cannes — the first South Korean filmmaker ever to win the category. He had previously won the Grand Prix for Oldboy (2004).
cannes.best_director.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Song Kang-ho becomes the first South Korean actor to win Best Actor at Cannes. Cer 2022 saw both Best Director and Best Actor go to South Korean talent — the country's strongest year at the festival.
cannes.best_actor.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Zar Amir Ebrahimi (Iranian-born French actress) wins Best Actress for Holy Spider, a film banned in Iran; Ebrahimi had been forced to leave Iran in 2008 after a private video was leaked publicly.
cannes.best_actress.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.note: Riley Keough's directorial debut (co-directed with Gina Gammell). Keough is better-known as an actress; her Caméra d'Or win is one of a small handful of high-profile actor-to-director crossovers honoured by Cannes.
cannes.camera_dor.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Justine Triet becomes only the third woman to win the Palme d'Or (after Jane Campion's shared 1993 win for The Piano and Julia Ducournau's solo 2021 win for Titane). Anatomy of a Fall went on to receive five Academy Award nominations (cer 96), winning Best Original Screenplay.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Jonathan Glazer's first Cannes prize. The Zone of Interest later won Best International Feature at the Oscars (cer 96).
cannes.grand_prix.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Aki Kaurismäki's first Jury Prize — the deadpan Finnish auteur returned to feature filmmaking after a six-year retirement.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Tran Anh Hung's first Best Director at Cannes — the French-Vietnamese filmmaker's first major Cannes prize since The Scent of Green Papaya took the Caméra d'Or in 1993.
cannes.best_director.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Kōji Yakusho's first Cannes Best Actor for Wim Wenders's Perfect Days — Yakusho was a long-standing collaborator of Shohei Imamura (Palme d'Or winner The Eel, Black Rain).
cannes.best_actor.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Merve Dizdar becomes the first Turkish actress to win Best Actress at Cannes.
cannes.best_actress.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.note: Pham Thien An's debut feature wins Caméra d'Or — Vietnam's first major win in the category in the post-2000 era.
cannes.camera_dor.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Sean Baker's first Palme d'Or for Anora — the film went on to win five Oscars including Best Picture, Director, Actress (Mikey Madison), Original Screenplay, and Editing at the 97th Academy Awards (cer 97). Neon's first Cannes Palme. Baker is the first American filmmaker to win the Palme d'Or since Terrence Malick's The Tree of Life in 2011.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Payal Kapadia's first feature Grand Prix — India's first Grand Prix at Cannes. The film was widely acclaimed as the first major Indian film selected in Competition at Cannes in three decades.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Jacques Audiard's third major Cannes prize (he previously won the Palme d'Or for Dheepan in 2015 and the Grand Prix for A Prophet in 2009). Emilia Pérez later set the record for most Oscar nominations for a non-English-language film (13) at the 97th Academy Awards.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Miguel Gomes's first Best Director at Cannes — the Portuguese filmmaker's most-prominent prize after years of festival circuit acclaim.
cannes.best_director.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Jesse Plemons's first Cannes Best Actor.
cannes.best_actor.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Best Actress awarded as a four-person team to the entire principal cast of Emilia Pérez (Adriana Paz, Karla Sofía Gascón, Selena Gomez, Zoe Saldaña). Karla Sofía Gascón becomes the first openly transgender performer to share a major Cannes acting prize. CSV split_co_recipients=true produces 4 rows for the team award; all 4 flagged co_winner=true. Phase E winner_recipient check uses the first row (Adriana Paz, alphabetical-first in the Wikipedia listing).
cannes.best_actress.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: Coralie Fargeat's first Cannes prize — The Substance went on to receive five Academy Award nominations (cer 97).
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.note: Halfdan Ullmann Tøndel — grandson of Ingmar Bergman and Liv Ullmann — wins Caméra d'Or for his debut feature. Norway's third Caméra d'Or.
cannes.camera_dor.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.winner_film: The Man Who Could Not Remain Silent
cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Jafar Panahi's first Palme d'Or — a landmark moment given Panahi had been banned from filmmaking by the Iranian regime for over a decade and had previously been imprisoned. Panahi attended the ceremony in person, his first international festival appearance after the lifting of his travel ban. The 78th Cannes Film Festival saw an exceptional density of ties — five categories tied (Best Director, Best Actor, Best Actress, Best Screenplay, and Jury Prize three-way).
cannes.palme_dor.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Joachim Trier's first Grand Prix — his first major Cannes prize after years of Competition selections.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Three-way tie — the second 3-way Jury Prize tie in modern Cannes history (after 2019). Oliver Laxe (Sirāt), Mascha Schilinski (Sound of Falling), and Simón Mesa Soto (A Poet) share. CSV co_winner flag inconsistency: only the first two rows have co_winner=true; the third (A Poet) has co_winner=false. See known-issue cannes-co-winner-flag-multi-bullet-tie-2026-05-04 — affects co_winner flag accuracy only, not winner identity or count.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

winner_films: Once Upon a Time in Gaza
cannes.best_director.note: Two-way tie. Kleber Mendonça Filho (Brazil) shares Best Director with the Palestinian twin brothers Tarzan and Arab Nasser. CSV co_winner=false on both rows because the wikitext format used separate top-level bullets per winner rather than the canonical sub-bullet tie format — same root cause as the cannes-co-winner-flag-multi-bullet-tie-2026-05-04 issue.
cannes.best_director.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Two-way tie. Wagner Moura becomes the first Brazilian actor to win Best Actor at Cannes; shares the prize with Frank Dillane (Urchin). Same multi-bullet-tie co_winner flag issue as Best Director.
cannes.best_actor.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

winner_films: I Only Rest in the Storm
cannes.best_actress.note: Two-way tie between Nadia Melliti (The Little Sister) and Cleo Diára (I Only Rest in the Storm). Same multi-bullet-tie co_winner flag issue as Best Director.
cannes.best_actress.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: Two-way tie. The Dardenne brothers (two-time Palme winners for Rosetta 1999 and L'Enfant 2005) share Best Screenplay with Harry Lighton (Pillion).
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com
